images.copyright: Plains zebra foal in Etosha National Park, Namibia (© Sharon Heald/Nature Picture Library)
images.title: Reading between the lines
images.caption: Plains zebra foal in Etosha National Park, Namibia
images.desc: Etosha National Park, Namibia, is not a place for soft starts, and the plains zebra foal in this image seems to get the memo. In a land this vast—nearly 8,600 square miles of salt pans and grassland—timing is everything, and hesitation is expensive. Lions, spotted hyenas, wild dogs, cheetahs, and leopards constantly pressure the herd—especially the youngest—so zebras are born ready. Minutes after arrival, they're upright. Hours later, they're moving.
